brands.name: Hudson Valley Swim
brands.notes: Swim-lesson brand founded 2003 in New York's Hudson Valley; franchising since 2022 using a low-overhead pool-rental model (franchisees rent existing pool space rather than build facilities). Note: the official consumer site is hvswim.com (hudsonvalleyswim.com appears parked); franchise recruiting site is hudsonvalleyswimfranchise.com.

riskFlags.title: Very new franchise system, small base
riskFlags.detail: Began franchising in 2022; the 2024 FDD showed only 10 total units (4 franchised, 6 company-owned). The brand has since grown to ~27 listed locations, but most franchise units are under two years old, so long-term unit economics are unproven.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback (2024 FDD data) / hvswim.com location directory

riskFlags.title: AUV figure circulating is not FDD-verified
riskFlags.detail: A ~$264,000 average-revenue figure appears on aggregator sites, but FranchisePayback states Hudson Valley Swim does not disclose average unit volume in its FDD, so no Item 19-verified revenue figure is available.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur lists $93,745-$121,995; FranchisePayback (2024 FDD) lists $98,345-$131,495 including the franchise fee

totalInvestmentMax.note: See totalInvestmentMin note re: FDD-source variance up to $131,495

royaltyPercent.note: FranchisePayback shows a 6%-8% range; 8% of gross sales is the commonly cited headline rate

adFundPercent.note: 2% national brand fund; franchisees also commit ~$3,000/month minimum local marketing per aggregator summaries

totalUnits.note: 27 listed locations (franchised + company-owned) across 11 states; Entrepreneur listed 20 units, 2024 FDD showed 10

canRunFromHome.note: Home-based/mobile operation permitted; lessons are held in rented pool space

isGrowing.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory (unit growth) / hvswim.com directory
isGrowing.note: Grew from 10 units (2024 FDD) to ~27 listed locations by 2026

brands.name: Jars by Fabio Viviani
brands.notes: Celebrity-chef dessert-jar concept launched via Fransmart; franchisor FVH Jars Franchise LLC formed Aug 2021, offering franchises since Jan 2022. The system has contracted sharply: at least five locations closed in 2025-2026 and the official site now lists only four locations, at least one of which (Saint Louis/Ladue) is independently reported closed.

riskFlags.title: Multiple unit closures in 2025-2026 (system contraction)
riskFlags.detail: Reported closures: Fort Worth, TX and Dallas, TX (Jan 2025, leaving 5 open per CultureMap); Overland Park, KS (Mar 2025, open less than a year); Ladue/St. Louis, MO (Apr 2026, after 19 months); Laguna Niguel, CA shown as closed on Yelp. The brand's own site still lists Saint Louis and Laguna Niguel, suggesting the location page is stale and true open count may be 2-3.
riskFlags.source: CultureMap Fort Worth; Johnson County Post; Hoodline / St. Louis Magazine

riskFlags.title: Very young celebrity-brand system with unproven economics
riskFlags.detail: Franchisor entity formed August 2021 and began offering franchises January 2022 through development agent Fransmart; the system never scaled past roughly 7-9 units before contracting, so there is no track record of sustained franchisee success.
riskFlags.source: Jars by Fabio Viviani 2022 FDD summary, The FDD Exchange

statesOpen.note: Emeryville listed on official site; the other CA listing (Laguna Niguel) is marked closed on Yelp

totalInvestmentMin.note: Single-unit range $208,950-$644,950; FDD also offered a 5-unit multi-unit program at $349,000-$785,500 incl. $140,000 development fee

franchiseFee.note: Single-unit fee; multi-unit development fee was $140,000 for 5 units per FDD

totalUnits.note: Site lists Chicago, Emeryville, Laguna Niguel, Saint Louis, but Saint Louis closed Apr 2026 and Laguna Niguel is marked closed on Yelp, so true open count may be 2-3; Entrepreneur listed 7 units

closureCount.source: CultureMap Fort Worth/Dallas, Johnson County Post, Hoodline, Yelp
closureCount.note: At least 5 reported closures 2025-2026: Fort Worth TX, Dallas TX, Overland Park KS, Ladue/St. Louis MO, Laguna Niguel CA

yearFounded.note: Franchisor entity FVH Jars Franchise LLC formed Aug 2021; Entrepreneur lists 2022 as founding year

yearFranchising.source: Jars by Fabio Viviani 2022 FDD (The FDD Exchange)

multiUnitAvailable.source: Jars by Fabio Viviani FDD summary (The FDD Exchange)
multiUnitAvailable.note: Multi-Unit Operator Agreement with 5-unit minimum was offered

isGrowing.source: CultureMap / Johnson County Post / Hoodline closure reporting
isGrowing.note: System contracted from ~9 to roughly 2-4 open locations across 2025-2026

brands.name: Jeff's Bagel Run
brands.notes: Orlando-born scratch-made bagel concept (started as a home bagel-delivery operation in 2019, first shop 2021) franchising since 2023. One of the fastest-growing emerging food brands: ~35 shops open by early 2026 with 120+ locations in development, including a 15-unit Utah deal.

riskFlags.title: Development pipeline far exceeds open-unit track record
riskFlags.detail: Only began franchising in 2023 (2025 FDD showed 11 units, 9 of them company-owned); by 2026 about 35 are open with 120+ in development. Most franchise units have under a year of operating history, so franchisee-level economics are largely unproven despite strong corporate-store sales.

riskFlags.title: High financial qualification bar
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists a $500,000 liquid-capital and $1,000,000 net-worth requirement, well above typical emerging fast-casual brands, reflecting the ~$538K-$814K build-out cost.

statesOpen.note: Home market; 9+ locations incl. Orlando and Tampa
Colorado Springs, first CO location, Feb 2026

avgUnitRevenue.source: Franchise Times reporting on 2025 FDD Item 19
avgUnitRevenue.note: Mean of the only two company-owned stores open all of 2024 ($1,252,496 and $841,884) per 2025 FDD Item 19; franchise units were too new for full-year figures (Item 19 also reports first-12-week avg weekly sales of $17,221-$19,369). Brand separately claims ~$1.2M AUV in trade press.

totalUnits.note: ~35 open with 120+ in development as of early 2026; 2025 FDD showed 11 units (9 company-owned, 2 franchised)

yearFounded.note: Began as a home bagel-delivery operation in 2019; first brick-and-mortar shop 2021 (FranchisePayback lists 2021)

item19Available.source: Franchise Times reporting on 2025 FDD Item 19

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (2024 FDD on file)

multiUnitAvailable.source: QSR Magazine (15-unit Utah development deal; 20-unit South/Mid-Atlantic awards)

isGrowing.source: Fast Casual / QSR Magazine / Franchise Times
isGrowing.note: 10 openings YTD 2026; ranked #124 on Entrepreneur's 2026 emerging-franchise list

brands.name: Joshua Tree Experts
brands.notes: Pennsylvania-based tree care, plant health care, lawn care and pest control franchise (franchising since 2021; franchise recruiting at jtefranchising.com / treecarefranchising.com). Recurring services make up over 60% of revenue; system grew from 1 franchised unit in 2022 to 36 open territories by mid-2025.

riskFlags.title: Item 19 relies heavily on company-owned operations
riskFlags.detail: The 2025 FDD Item 19 features the mature company-owned Stockertown, PA operation ($6.66M gross sales across 3 territories in 2024) and Souderton ($782,548 across 2 territories); the 2024 FDD's only full-year franchisee figure was $171,215 in first-full-year sales (2022). Franchisee results may differ substantially from corporate flagship results, especially given the ~$460K-$597K investment.
riskFlags.source: PeerSense (2025 FDD) / Franchise Chatter FDD Talk (2024 FDD)

riskFlags.title: Young franchise system with awarded-vs-open gap
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2021; as of June 2025, 47 territories were awarded but 36 open across 16 franchisees plus 5 company-owned units. Royalty structure also changed between FDDs (2024 FDD: greater of 7% or monthly minimums; 2025 FDD: performance-tiered 6% down to 3.5%).

statesOpen.note: Home state: Allentown/Stockertown HQ plus multiple service areas

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $460,669-$597,460 with equipment purchased outright; vehicles/machinery are $324K-$382K of that, and prior FDD language showed a much lower upfront range (~$196,760-$303,091) when equipment is financed

franchiseFee.note: First territory; $42,000 second, $38,000 third (2024 FDD listed $49,900)

royaltyPercent.note: Performance-tiered from 6% down to 3.5% of gross sales per 2025 FDD; the 2024 FDD used greater of 7% or monthly minimums ($500-$1,500/mo)

adFundPercent.note: 1% brand development fund; plus local marketing minimum of $4,000/month initially and 6.5% of gross revenue from month 12

totalUnits.note: 36 territories open (47 awarded) across 16 franchisees, plus 5 company-owned units

veteranDiscount.note: 10% off standard franchise fee for military veterans

item19Available.note: Item 19 discloses revenue by service line; figures are dominated by company-owned operations

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (2023 FDD on file)

multiUnitAvailable.source: PeerSense (2025 FDD: discounted second/third territory fees)

isGrowing.source: PeerSense (2025 FDD) vs Franchise Chatter (2024 FDD: 1 franchised unit in 2022)
isGrowing.note: From 1 franchised territory in 2022 to 36 open / 47 awarded by June 2025

brands.name: Junbi
brands.notes: California-based Japanese matcha and tea cafe chain (formerly Sip Matcha), in business since 2017 and franchising since 2020. Small but expanding system: 2024 FDD showed 7 units (5 franchised, 2 company-owned), with 2025-2026 openings in the Bay Area, Seattle, and a Hawaii franchise group, plus stated interest in Canada/Asia development.

riskFlags.title: Small system with slow early growth
riskFlags.detail: Franchising since 2020 but only 7 total units in the 2024 FDD (5 franchised, 2 company-owned). Growth has picked up in 2025-2026 (Livermore, Mountain View, San Francisco, Seattle, Hawaii), but the system remains very small and matcha is a trend-exposed category.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback (2024 FDD) / ABC7 San Francisco / Wikipedia

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 revenue disclosure
riskFlags.detail: FranchisePayback notes Junbi does not disclose average unit volume (average revenue) in its FDD, so prospective franchisees cannot validate unit economics from the disclosure document.

statesOpen.note: Home market: Livermore, Mountain View, San Francisco and SoCal origins
Franchise group led by David So, Joe Jitsukawa and Greg Kobashigawa

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum liquid cash funds to be considered; can include financing, securities, 401k/IRA rollovers

totalInvestmentMin.note: Varies by geography and real-estate type; 2024 FDD range was $268,750-$576,250

adFundPercent.note: Brand fund currently 1% (cap up to 2%), plus 2% local marketing requirement

totalUnits.note: 5 franchised + 2 company-owned per 2024 FDD; additional locations opened 2025-2026 (Seattle, Bay Area, Hawaii) so current count is higher

trainingWeeks.note: Approximately one week for Managing Owner plus one manager, in Los Angeles or virtual, completed within 45 days before opening

exclusiveTerritories.note: Protected territory, typically a two-mile radius around the shop, adjusted for density

isGrowing.note: New units in Livermore, Mountain View, San Francisco, Seattle, Hawaii; exploring Canada and Asia
